articles.summary: Google's Gemma 4 LLM is now available on Docker Hub as OCI artifacts, packaged in three architectures scaling from edge to high-performance servers. Models become versioned, shareable, and deployable directly into CI/CD pipelines; Docker Model Runner support is coming to enable discovery, management, and deployment from Docker Desktop.

articles.title: Docker Offload now Generally Available: The Full Power of Docker, for Every Developer, Everywhere.
articles.summary: Docker Offload, a managed cloud service that runs the container engine in Docker's cloud, is now generally available. Developers retain familiar CLI and Docker Desktop UI while the engine moves to the cloud, enabling Docker Desktop to work in restricted environments like VDI and managed desktops that previously blocked it.

articles.title: Credit cards are vulnerable to brute force kind attacks
articles.summary: Research demonstrates that credit card payment systems remain susceptible to brute-force attacks despite existing security measures. The article explores the attack vectors and implications for payment infrastructure security.

articles.title: AWS Transform now automates BI migration to Amazon Quick in days
articles.summary: AWS Transform automates migration of legacy BI dashboards to Amazon QuickSight, reducing timelines from months to days by preserving calculated fields, layouts, and security rules while enabling serverless scale and native AI capabilities. The service integrates partner agents through AWS Marketplace to handle dashboard transformation at scale.

articles.title: Flock cameras keep telling police a man who doesn't have a warrant has a warrant
articles.summary: Investigation into Flock Security camera system providing false warrant information to law enforcement. Raises concerns about accuracy and reliability of automated surveillance systems used in policing.

articles.title: AI Uses Less Water Than the Public Thinks
articles.summary: Analysis of AI infrastructure water consumption in California, arguing public perception overestimates actual usage. Provides context for data center sustainability and operational costs of LLM inference at scale.

articles.title: Show HN: Site Mogging
articles.summary: A demo project combining Cloudflare Workers AI and Browser Run to compare websites using Google's Gemma 4b vision model. Shows practical application of edge-deployed vision models for automated web analysis.

articles.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Get 4-Year Sentences in BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
articles.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice sentenced Ryan Goldberg and Kevin Martin to four years in prison for deploying BlackCat ransomware against multiple U.S. victims between April and December 2023. The case highlights criminal prosecution of insiders facilitating ransomware operations.

articles.title: Show HN: Perfect Bluetooth MIDI for Windows
articles.summary: Open-source utility bridging Bluetooth LE MIDI keyboards into Windows MIDI Services, solving three stacked bugs: WinRT API exposure, loopback endpoint routing, and bidirectional note transmission. Uses Windows MIDI Services loopback endpoints to provide DAW compatibility.

articles.title: Does Postgres Scale?
articles.summary: Benchmarking study on PostgreSQL's scalability for workflow execution. Evaluates whether Postgres can handle distributed workflow orchestration at scale.

articles.title: I built a Game Boy emulator in F#
articles.summary: Technical writeup on implementing a Game Boy emulator in F#, covering CPU emulation, memory management, and graphics rendering.

articles.title: Speed Up Unreal Engine NNE Inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for RTX Runtime
articles.summary: NVIDIA demonstrates TensorRT optimization for Unreal Engine 5's Neural Network Engine, accelerating inference for graphics tasks like super resolution, denoising, and neural rendering on RTX GPUs.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA released DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation, Multi Frame Generation 6X, and a second-generation transformer model for Super Resolution, enabling game developers to integrate AI-driven upscaling and frame generation in Unreal Engine 5.

articles.title: Show HN: TRiP – a complete transformer engine in C built from scratch just by me
articles.summary: TRiP is a transformer inference engine implemented in C from scratch, providing a minimal but complete implementation of transformer architecture for educational and practical use.

articles.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
articles.summary: NVIDIA provides guidance on building and scaling generative AI workflows in ComfyUI, an open-source node-based tool that runs on RTX GPUs and integrates image generation, video synthesis, and language models into repeatable pipelines for creative teams.

articles.title: Meta abandons open-source Llama for proprietary Muse Spark
articles.summary: Meta has deprioritized Llama development in favor of proprietary Muse Spark, built by Meta Superintelligence Labs with new infrastructure and architecture. Zuckerberg reportedly wanted a model competitive with ChatGPT and Claude, leading to a complete rebuild rather than Llama iteration.

articles.title: Securing the last mile with local account password rotation
articles.summary: IBM Vault Enterprise 2.0 introduced a plugin for automated rotation of local OS account passwords on RHEL, Ubuntu, and other systems. This addresses the security gap of unmanaged local accounts that fall outside centralized identity providers, bringing them under centralized control, rotation, and audit standards.

articles.title: I benchmarked Claude Code's caveman plugin against "be brief."
articles.summary: A benchmark comparing Anthropic's Claude Code caveman plugin (a tool-use feature for concise output) against a simple prompt instruction to be brief. The analysis evaluates whether specialized tool-use mechanisms outperform basic prompt engineering for controlling LLM verbosity.

articles.title: Approximate Answers, Exact Decisions: New Sketch Functions for Analytics
articles.summary: Databricks introduces sketch functions for analytics that provide approximate answers for decision-support queries, enabling faster analysis when exact precision is not required for business decisions.

articles.title: Companies Winning with AI Built the Data Layer First
articles.summary: Successful AI deployments prioritize foundational data infrastructure and governance before building ML models or agents. The article emphasizes that competitive advantage in AI comes from data quality and accessibility, not just model capability.

articles.title: Ramp's Sheets AI Exfiltrates Financials
articles.summary: A security research article documenting how Ramp's Sheets AI feature can exfiltrate financial data through prompt injection or similar attack vectors. The article appears to be a case study in AI security vulnerabilities in enterprise tools.

articles.title: FastCGI: 30 years old and still the better protocol for reverse proxies
articles.summary: This post argues that FastCGI remains superior to HTTP for reverse proxy communication due to its binary protocol efficiency, connection pooling, and lower overhead compared to HTTP-based alternatives. The author contends that despite its age, FastCGI's design choices make it better suited for high-performance proxy scenarios than modern protocols.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They're Built
articles.summary: IBM details the architecture and training methodology behind Granite 4.1, a family of foundation models. The post covers model design choices, training infrastructure, and performance characteristics relevant to practitioners building on these models.

articles.title: Interoperability Between Unity Catalog and Google BigQuery via Catalog Federation
articles.summary: Databricks enables catalog federation between Unity Catalog and Google BigQuery, allowing customers to access the same data copy across both platforms for unified data governance.

articles.title: Letting AI play my game – building an agentic test harness to help play-testing
articles.summary: A developer built an agentic test harness using AI to automate play-testing of their game. The approach demonstrates practical tool-use for game QA, where agents can systematically explore game states and behavior.

articles.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
articles.summary: The Hacker News reports on a February 2026 discovery of threat actors using custom AI agents to automate attacks, including Active Directory mapping and credential theft at scale. The webinar addresses the gap between AI-accelerated attacks and defensive workflows.

articles.title: Building AI-ready data: Vanguard's Virtual Analyst journey
articles.summary: Vanguard deployed a conversational AI system to let financial analysts query complex datasets without SQL expertise, reducing request turnaround from days to minutes. The implementation focused on eight guiding principles for AI-ready data infrastructure, including semantic context and metadata management, using AWS services to enable immediate analyst responses.

articles.title: What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
articles.summary: The article examines how exposure management platforms often fail to provide meaningful context beyond patch counts and CVSS scores, leaving security teams unable to answer whether their organizations are actually safer. It critiques the gap between vulnerability closure metrics and real risk reduction.

articles.title: Low-Compilation-Cost Register Allocation in LLVM-Based Binary Translation
articles.summary: ACM research paper on optimizing register allocation for LLVM binary translation to reduce compilation overhead. Relevant for compiler infrastructure and systems engineers working on translation layers.

articles.title: Don't Panic: The Thymeleaf Template Injection That Only Hurts If You Let It (CVE-2026-40478)
articles.summary: CVE-2026-40478 is a Thymeleaf template injection (CVSS 9.1) that requires dynamic view or template expression misuse to be exploitable. Snyk recommends patching to 3.1.4+ and auditing code for unsafe dynamic template handling.

articles.title: Before GitHub
articles.summary: Historical retrospective on version control and collaboration workflows before GitHub's dominance, covering CVS, Subversion, and early distributed systems. Contextualizes how modern development practices evolved and what was lost in the transition.

articles.title: "Developer loyalty is at zero right now": Google doesn't care which AI coding tool you use
articles.summary: Google Cloud's strategy is to be the best platform for running AI code and models regardless of which coding tool developers choose. Google claims Anthropic's Claude performs better on Vertex than on Azure, AWS, or Anthropic's own platform, positioning Google Cloud as the optimal runtime for any AI development workflow.

articles.title: Operationalizing AI for public sector fraud prevention
articles.summary: This piece discusses deploying AI systems for fraud detection in government agencies, addressing the operational challenges of moving from pilots to production. It covers model deployment, data pipelines, and governance considerations specific to public sector constraints.

articles.title: Why Broadcom is betting on a private cloud comeback
articles.summary: Broadcom's VMware Cloud Foundation is evolving to balance ease-of-use with open, Kubernetes-aligned flexibility as enterprises shift workloads back to private clouds for data sovereignty. VCF integrates Kubernetes as core infrastructure while maintaining open-source alignment, supporting platform engineering teams at scale.

articles.title: Brazilian LofyGang Resurfaces After Three Years With Minecraft LofyStealer Campaign
articles.summary: The Brazilian cybercrime group LofyGang has returned after three years with a new stealer malware called LofyStealer (GrabBot) targeting Minecraft players. The malware disguises itself as a legitimate Minecraft hack called 'Slinky' using the official game icon to trick users into execution.

articles.title: Show HN: Auto-Architecture: Karpathy's Loop, pointed at a CPU
articles.summary: A project implementing automated CPU architecture design using Karpathy's loop methodology. This demonstrates practical application of AI-driven optimization for hardware architecture exploration.

articles.title: Turning secret detection into measurable risk reduction
articles.summary: HashiCorp Vault Radar correlates exposed secrets with centralized secret stores (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) to distinguish between unmanaged exposure and already-controlled secrets, providing context for prioritized remediation. The tool moves beyond detection to build mature workflows with accountability tracking, multi-team coordination, and measurable progress metrics across security, development, and operations.

articles.title: 24/7 Simulation Loops: How Agentic AI Keeps Subsurface Engineering Moving
articles.summary: NVIDIA explores agentic AI for subsurface engineering, automating 24/7 simulation workflows to overcome manual data overhead and human bandwidth bottlenecks in reservoir modeling. The approach enables on-demand, continuous simulation loops without expert intervention.

articles.title: Lovelace emerges from stealth with context engine that claims 1000x AI investigative power
articles.summary: Lovelace AI, founded by Andrew Moore (former Google Cloud AI head and Carnegie Mellon dean), launches Elemental, an enterprise context engine designed for high-stakes investigative questions that require joining millions of data points across petabytes. Moore distinguishes between tasks where LLMs excel (summarization, conversational queries) and investigative contexts where they consistently fail, positioning Elemental to bridge that gap.

articles.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
articles.summary: CVE-2026-32202 (CVSS 4.3), a Windows Shell spoofing vulnerability patched in Patch Tuesday, is now confirmed under active exploitation in the wild. The flaw allows attackers to access sensitive information through shell interaction.

articles.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
articles.summary: OpenAI GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents are now available on AWS, enabling enterprises to build and deploy AI applications in their AWS environments with security controls.

articles.title: Microsoft-OpenAI rewrite opens the door for Anthropic and Google
articles.summary: OpenAI and Microsoft restructured their partnership agreement, allowing OpenAI to deliver services across cloud platforms beyond Azure while maintaining revenue-share payments to Microsoft through 2030. The change signals OpenAI's intent to broaden enterprise reach across multiple cloud hyperscalers and Microsoft's parallel push to develop its own AI models and expand Microsoft 365 Copilot offerings.

articles.title: Build and deploy an automatic sync solution for Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
articles.summary: This post describes an event-driven serverless architecture for automatically syncing S3 documents to Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases while respecting service quotas and rate limits. The solution detects S3 events, triggers ingestion jobs, and provides comprehensive monitoring to keep knowledge bases current for RAG applications without manual intervention.

articles.title: How Popsa used Amazon Nova to inspire customers with personalised title suggestions
articles.summary: Popsa integrated Amazon Nova Lite and Pro models via Amazon Bedrock to generate multilingual photo book titles and subtitles using metadata, computer vision, and RAG. The unified Bedrock API reduced latency, improved quality, and cut costs compared to prior approaches, demonstrating practical cost-benefit tradeoffs in production generative AI.

articles.title: "I was tired of explaining it to somebody who was supposed to build it for me": Meet the executives vibe-coding their own tools.
articles.summary: Executives and C-suite leaders are using Claude, Cursor, and embedded AI features to build production agents and automation tools without traditional developers. Moshe Bar, CEO of Codenotary, built a 140,000-line BBS system for IBM 3270 mainframe terminals using Claude, personally editing only 10 lines, with 500 users and zero downtime.

articles.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
articles.summary: Checkmarx's investigation into its March 23, 2026 supply-chain attack reveals that threat actors accessed and exfiltrated data from the company's GitHub repository, later posting it on the dark web. The initial compromise was facilitated through the supply-chain attack vector.

articles.title: Can your operations handle your security ambitions?
articles.summary: Enterprise security teams often struggle to operationalize new security tools because legacy systems, manual workflows, and misalignment between security and engineering stacks create friction. The article warns that adding sophisticated security tooling without addressing underlying operational complexity can turn security into a bottleneck rather than an enabler.

articles.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
articles.summary: Pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group PhantomCore has been actively targeting TrueConf video conferencing servers in Russia since September 2025 using an exploit chain of three vulnerabilities to achieve remote code execution. Positive Technologies documented the campaign, detailing how the threat actors chain multiple CVEs in the conferencing software to compromise susceptible instances.

articles.title: Inside one of the first production deployments of Lakebase: LangGuard's agentic workflow governance engine
articles.summary: Databricks showcases LangGuard's use of Lakebase to build governance controls for autonomous AI agents. The case study highlights how Lakebase enables monitoring, auditing, and policy enforcement for multi-step agentic workflows at scale.

articles.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
articles.summary: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended partnership agreement that clarifies long-term commitments and governance. The deal simplifies the relationship while supporting continued AI infrastructure investment and innovation.

articles.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
articles.summary: Tutorial on integrating OpenAI's Privacy Filter into web applications to handle sensitive data at scale. Covers practical implementation patterns for filtering PII and other sensitive information before sending requests to LLM APIs.

articles.title: The next generation of Databricks Genie
articles.summary: Databricks announces an updated Genie product with improved natural language-to-SQL capabilities, faster query generation, and enhanced reasoning over complex schemas. The new version targets both analysts and data engineers for self-service analytics.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick connects marketing systems and tools into a personal knowledge graph that answers questions grounded in real business data and executes tasks autonomously. The platform reduces manual data assembly across multiple sources, enabling campaign performance intelligence and team-wide insights without context-switching.

articles.title: Plugins and skills
articles.summary: Tutorial on Codex plugins and skills for connecting external tools, accessing data, and building repeatable workflows. Demonstrates how to chain tool calls for task automation.

articles.title: Automations
articles.summary: Guide to scheduling and triggering automations in Codex for recurring tasks like report generation and summarization. Shows how to eliminate manual execution overhead.

articles.title: What is Codex?
articles.summary: Introductory guide to Codex, OpenAI's agent framework for automating tasks beyond chat. Covers how Codex connects tools and produces concrete outputs like documents and dashboards.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Guide to running Transformers.js models directly in Chrome extensions for client-side inference without server calls. Covers practical setup, model loading, and performance considerations for in-browser NLP tasks.

articles.title: Simplify Sparse Deep Learning with Universal Sparse Tensor in nvmath-python
articles.summary: Universal Sparse Tensor (UST) integration into nvmath-python v0.9.0 decouples tensor sparsity from memory layout, enabling developers to optimize sparse scientific and deep learning workloads with greater flexibility. The feature targets performance improvements for sparse tensor operations without layout-specific constraints.

articles.title: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
articles.summary: Kubernetes v1.36 finalizes SELinuxMount feature for GA, with v1.37 expected to enable it by default. The change speeds up volume setup for most workloads but may break applications sharing volumes between privileged and unprivileged pods on the same node; operators running SELinux in enforcing mode should audit clusters in v1.36 before the breaking change.

articles.title: It's all about the angle: Your photos, re-composed
articles.summary: Google Research presents a generative AI approach to automatically recompose photographs by adjusting framing and perspective. The method learns optimal compositional angles from large image datasets and can apply professional framing principles to user photos without manual editing.

articles.title: Terraform adds pre-written Sentinel policies for ISO 27001
articles.summary: HashiCorp and AWS co-created pre-written Sentinel policies for AWS that map to ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A controls, covering access control, cryptography, logging, and secure configuration. These policies are now available in the Terraform Registry, lowering the barrier to policy-as-code adoption by eliminating the need to manually translate compliance frameworks into enforceable rules.

articles.title: From developer desks to the whole organization: Running Claude Cowork in Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Claude Cowork is now available in Amazon Bedrock, bringing multi-step research, document analysis, file processing, and report generation to knowledge workers via a desktop application. Data remains under customer control with no storage of prompts, files, or model responses; Anthropic-hosted features like Computer Use are available separately.

articles.title: Highlights from Git 2.54
articles.summary: Git 2.54 introduces the new experimental `git history` command for simpler history rewrites, supporting `reword` and `split` operations without the complexity of interactive rebase. The release includes contributions from 137 developers, 66 of them new.

articles.title: From public static void main to Golden Kubestronaut: The Art of unlearning
articles.summary: A developer's personal account of transitioning from traditional Java development to cloud-native architecture, holding all five Kubernetes certifications and achieving CNCF Golden Kubestronaut status. The piece emphasizes that the core challenge isn't learning new tools but unlearning traditional infrastructure assumptions—moving from siloed development to owning operational concerns like pod failures and YAML configuration.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: NVIDIA's AI physics approach accelerates design of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV nuclear designs by balancing safety, efficiency, and economics. The work applies physics-informed AI to standardize reactor designs and shift construction to controlled manufacturing.

articles.title: K3s on On-Prem Infrastructures the GitOps Way: Writing a Custom k0rdent Template from Scratch
articles.summary: Walkthrough of provisioning K3s clusters on Proxmox using k0rdent's Bring Your Own Template (BYOT) approach with custom Helm charts, enabling declarative, repeatable on-premise cluster lifecycle management without manual VM creation or bash scripts.

articles.title: AI-generated synthetic neurons speed up brain mapping
articles.summary: Researchers use generative AI to create synthetic neuron models that accelerate connectome reconstruction and brain mapping workflows. The approach reduces manual annotation burden while maintaining accuracy in mapping neural circuits.

articles.title: AWS Interconnect is now generally available, with a new option to simplify last-mile connectivity
articles.summary: AWS Interconnect GA adds managed private connectivity for multicloud and hybrid environments, with two new capabilities: Interconnect-multicloud for VPC-to-VPC connections across cloud providers, and Interconnect-last-mile for simplified high-speed private connections from branch offices and data centers. The service reduces networking overhead by enabling resilient, end-to-end connectivity configuration through the AWS Console without manual VPN and colocation management.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVbandwidth is a profiling tool for measuring GPU memory and interconnect performance in CUDA applications, supporting both single-GPU and multi-GPU systems. The tool helps developers understand data transfer bottlenecks critical to application performance.

articles.title: ingress-nginx to Envoy Gateway migration on CNCF internal services cluster
articles.summary: CNCF migrated its internal services cluster from Ingress NGINX to Envoy Gateway, leveraging the Gateway API's multi-layer architecture to reduce load balancer costs by consolidating multiple Gateways into shared Gateway objects with HTTPRoute/TLSRoute configuration.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 improves interactive 3D world generation with better fidelity while maintaining efficiency on consumer GPUs. The release focuses on optimizations for real-time rendering and agent simulation environments.

articles.title: Improving the academic workflow: Introducing two AI agents for better figures and peer review
articles.summary: Google Research presents two specialized AI agents: one for automatically improving scientific figure quality and another for assisting with peer review processes. The agents aim to streamline academic publishing workflows and reduce manual effort.

articles.title: Reclaim Developer Hours through Smarter Vulnerability Prioritization with Docker and Mend.io
articles.summary: Docker Hardened Images now integrate with Mend.io to automatically distinguish between base image and application-layer vulnerabilities using VEX statements. The zero-config integration marks DHI-protected packages in the Mend UI and filters noise by combining risk factors with reachability analysis to surface only exploitable CVEs.

articles.title: A Report on Burnout in Open Source Software Communities (2025) [pdf]
articles.summary: A research report examining burnout patterns and contributing factors within open source software communities, based on 2025 data.

articles.title: Show HN: My Private GitHub on Postgres
articles.summary: A self-hosted Git repository implementation backed by PostgreSQL instead of traditional file-based storage. Trades off some Git compatibility for a queryable, relational backend suitable for private deployments.

articles.title: The Gay Jailbreak Technique
articles.summary: Prompt injection technique targeting LLM safety guardrails. Demonstrates a jailbreak method that exploits model behavior around sensitive topics to bypass content policies.

articles.title: Spotify adds 'Verified' badges to distinguish human artists from AI
articles.summary: Spotify is introducing verified badges to help listeners identify human artists versus AI-generated content on the platform. This addresses growing concerns about AI-generated music flooding streaming services and helps maintain artist authenticity and discoverability.

articles.title: whohas – Command-line utility for cross-distro, cross-repository package search
articles.summary: whohas is a CLI tool that searches for packages across multiple Linux distributions and package repositories in a single query. It simplifies finding which distro carries a specific package version without checking each repository individually.

articles.title: Running Adobe's 1991 PostScript Interpreter in the Browser
articles.summary: A technical deep-dive on porting a 35-year-old PostScript interpreter to run in the browser, likely using WebAssembly or JavaScript transpilation. Demonstrates feasibility of running legacy binary code in modern web environments.

articles.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber
articles.summary: OpenAI is restricting access to its Cyber model after previously criticizing Anthropic for limiting access to Mythos. Both companies are now gating advanced capabilities, signaling a shift toward restricted deployment of frontier models.

articles.title: A beginner's guide to Sourcehut (2025)
articles.summary: Introductory guide to Sourcehut, a Git-centric forge and CI/CD platform emphasizing simplicity and email-based workflows. Covers basic setup and usage for developers new to the platform.

articles.title: Show HN: Winpodx – run Windows apps on Linux as native windows
articles.summary: Winpodx is a tool enabling Windows applications to run on Linux as native windows, likely leveraging container or virtualization techniques. The project demonstrates cross-platform application compatibility but lacks sufficient detail on architecture or implementation approach from the excerpt alone.

articles.title: SimpleX Channels, SimpleX Network Consortium and Community Crowdfunding
articles.summary: SimpleX releases v6.5 with channels feature and announces network consortium and community crowdfunding initiative. Focus on decentralized messaging infrastructure and governance model.

articles.title: Unlocking SAP Business Context in Databricks with Semantic Metadata Delta Sharing
articles.summary: Databricks integrates semantic metadata capabilities with Delta Sharing to help organizations correlate and contextualize SAP data across systems. This enables better data discovery and lineage tracking for enterprise data platforms.

articles.title: Full-Text Search with DuckDB
articles.summary: Tutorial on implementing full-text search capabilities using DuckDB, an in-process SQL database engine.

articles.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Interactive v. non-interactive mode
articles.summary: Tutorial on GitHub Copilot CLI modes: interactive mode for iterative chat-like sessions and non-interactive mode for one-off queries. Covers how to launch each mode and typical use cases for command-line assistance.

articles.title: Claude Code refuses requests or charges extra if your commits mention "OpenClaw"
articles.summary: Report that Anthropic's Claude Code tool appears to refuse requests or apply additional charges when commit messages reference 'OpenClaw'. Raises questions about conditional behavior in AI coding assistants.

articles.title: Alert Fatigue Is a Business Risk
articles.summary: Databricks examines alert fatigue in security operations centers as a material business risk and explores how data infrastructure can reduce noise through better threat intelligence and security analytics at scale.

articles.title: I aggregated 28 US Government auction sites into one search
articles.summary: A developer built a search aggregator across US government auction sites. Practical web scraping and search indexing project with modest engineering interest.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: DeepMind outlines research into AI co-clinician systems designed to augment clinical decision-making. The work explores how AI models can be integrated into healthcare workflows to support rather than replace clinicians.

articles.title: Backstage with Lakebase
articles.summary: Databricks explores the convergence of operational and analytical databases through Lakebase. The post discusses architectural patterns for unifying transactional and analytical workloads in a lakehouse context.

articles.title: Mozilla's Opposition to Chrome's Prompt API
articles.summary: Mozilla filed a standards position opposing Chrome's proposed Prompt API, likely citing concerns around user privacy, security, or web platform fragmentation. The GitHub issue generated significant discussion (103 comments, 275 points) but the excerpt does not detail Mozilla's specific objections or the API's technical scope.

articles.title: Functional programmers need to take a look at Zig
articles.summary: An argument for why functional programmers should explore Zig as a systems language. The post likely covers Zig's memory safety guarantees and how they align with functional programming principles.

articles.title: The Zig project's rationale for their firm anti-AI contribution policy
articles.summary: The Zig programming language project has adopted a strict policy against AI-generated contributions. The post outlines the project's reasoning for this stance and its implications for open-source governance.

articles.title: A Grounded Conceptual Model for Ownership Types in Rust
articles.summary: ACM research highlights a grounded conceptual model for Rust's ownership type system, providing formal foundations for understanding memory safety guarantees in the language.

articles.title: Mike: open-source legal AI
articles.summary: Mike is an open-source AI system designed for legal document analysis and reasoning. The project applies LLM capabilities to the legal domain with an emphasis on transparency and accessibility.

articles.title: Four ways Google Research scientists have been using Empirical Research Assistance
articles.summary: Google Research scientists apply Empirical Research Assistance to data mining and modeling tasks, demonstrating practical use cases for AI-assisted research workflows.

articles.title: I accidentally made law enforcement shut down their fake honeypot
articles.summary: A security researcher describes triggering an unintended shutdown of a law enforcement honeypot through DDoS activity. The post explores the technical and legal implications of disrupting a fake infrastructure meant to catch attackers.

articles.title: Rethinking SQL ETL for modern data platforms
articles.summary: SQL remains central to data transformation workflows on modern platforms, with evolving patterns for ETL pipelines. The article discusses how SQL-based approaches adapt to contemporary data platform architectures.

articles.title: At Protocol: Building the Social Internet
articles.summary: AT Protocol is a decentralized social networking protocol designed to enable interoperable social platforms without centralized control. The protocol allows users to own their data and move between applications while maintaining their identity and social graph.

articles.title: Stripe data now available on Databricks via Databricks Marketplace
articles.summary: Databricks Marketplace now offers Stripe payment data connectors, enabling users to ingest transaction and customer data directly into their Databricks workspace. This reduces integration effort for analytics on payment flows.

articles.title: Zed is 1.0

articles.title: Show HN: Adblock-rust Manager – Firefox extension to enable the Brave ad blocker
articles.summary: A Firefox extension provides UI controls for adblock-rust, Brave's Rust-based ad filtering engine that ships disabled in Firefox 149 with no WebExtension API access. The tool exposes filter list management and ETP toggle functionality via browser.privacy API and preset filter configurations.

articles.title: HashiCorp co-founder says GitHub 'no longer a place for serious work'
articles.summary: Mitchell Hashimoto announced moving Ghostty (his terminal emulator project) off GitHub, citing platform concerns. While primarily commentary, this reflects real developer friction with GitHub's direction and signals potential shifts in where open-source projects host infrastructure.

articles.title: HardenedBSD Is Now Officially on Radicle
articles.summary: HardenedBSD project has migrated to Radicle, a decentralized version control platform. This represents a shift away from centralized Git hosting toward peer-to-peer code collaboration infrastructure.

articles.title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
articles.summary: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age, focused on democratizing AI-powered cyber defense. The plan addresses protecting critical systems through broader adoption of AI-driven security tools and practices.

articles.title: Bridging the Gap to Autonomous Fixes: Snyk and Atlassian Unveil Intelligent Remediation for Jira
articles.summary: Snyk and Atlassian integrated Snyk Studio AI into Jira to auto-generate security fixes from vulnerability tickets, reducing context switching and remediation time. The integration uses AI agents to convert security findings into actionable code patches.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra is now integrated into Hugging Face's inference provider ecosystem, offering managed inference endpoints for model deployment. This expands the inference options available through the Hugging Face platform.

articles.title: Built In, Not Bolted On: What AI-Native Actually Means in Cybersecurity
articles.summary: The article examines how AI-native architecture differs from bolted-on AI in cybersecurity, emphasizing conversational analytics to reduce BI bottlenecks. It addresses the paradox cybersecurity companies face in operationalizing AI at scale.

articles.title: From system of record to system of control: How NetBox Labs is making network engineers "masters of intent."
articles.summary: NetBox Labs is positioning its platform as a system of control for network infrastructure, moving beyond spreadsheets and dashboards to provide a reliable representation of network state as infrastructure sprawls across regions and cloud providers. The company argues that modern networks require architects who understand the big picture rather than terminal wizards, especially as AI agents increase pressure on infrastructure reliability.

articles.title: Securely send first-party conversion signals with Snapchat Conversions API on Databricks Marketplace
articles.summary: Databricks Marketplace now integrates Snapchat Conversions API, allowing advertisers to send first-party conversion data (purchases, sign-ups) directly from their data platform for campaign optimization. This enables closed-loop measurement without third-party cookies.

articles.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
articles.summary: Research from the Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace report (surveying 500 security leaders) identifies secure data movement as a critical gap in Zero Trust implementations. The article argues that simply connecting systems and pushing data through gateways does not solve the underlying security problem, causing Zero Trust programs to stall.

articles.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
articles.summary: Xu Zewei, 34, a member of the Chinese state-sponsored Silk Typhoon group, was extradited from Italy to face charges for cyberattacks against U.S. organizations and government agencies between February 2020 and June 2021, including targeting COVID research.

articles.title: Our commitment to community safety
articles.summary: OpenAI describes its safety mechanisms for ChatGPT including model safeguards, misuse detection, and policy enforcement. The approach combines technical controls with collaboration with external safety experts.

articles.title: How leading tech companies are killing the builder's tax with Lakebase
articles.summary: Databricks positions Lakebase as a solution to reduce operational overhead in AI app development. The post argues that hidden infrastructure costs compound across data pipelines, governance, and model serving, and that unified data platforms can lower total cost of ownership for AI-native applications.

articles.title: GitHub moves Copilot to usage-based billing as AI coding costs climb
articles.summary: GitHub is shifting Copilot from fixed monthly fees with premium request units to a credit-based model tied to token consumption, effective June 1. Each plan includes a monthly allotment of GitHub AI credits with overage charges, aligning pricing with actual usage as the company manages demand and seeks a sustainable business model.

articles.title: Automate repetitive tasks with Amazon Quick Flows
articles.summary: Amazon Quick Flows automates repetitive business tasks using natural language descriptions without requiring coding or ML expertise. The system builds AI workflows from plain-language specifications, demonstrated through financial analysis and employee onboarding examples, enabling teams to redirect hours spent on manual data copying and formatting toward strategic work.

articles.title: What the 2026 State of Open Source report reveals about digital autonomy
articles.summary: The 2026 State of Open Source Report from Perforce OpenLogic shows that 55 percent of respondents cite avoiding vendor lock-in as a primary driver of open source adoption, up 68 percent year-over-year, with 63 percent in Europe. Organizations are increasingly viewing open source as a mechanism for control and long-term leverage as licensing models and regulatory mandates shift.

articles.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
articles.summary: OpenAI achieves FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling U.S. federal agencies to adopt AI services with compliance assurance.

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
articles.summary: Weekly roundup covering Fast16 malware, supply chain incidents, fake help desk attacks, malicious browser extensions, credential theft, and abuse of remote tools. The recap highlights recurring attack patterns and legacy vulnerabilities still being exploited.

articles.title: Fake CAPTCHA IRSF Scam and 120 Keitaro Campaigns Drive Global SMS, Crypto Fraud
articles.summary: Researchers uncovered a telecommunications fraud operation using fake CAPTCHA verification to trick users into sending international SMS messages that generate charges on their mobile bills, with 120 Keitaro campaigns identified as part of the scheme. The threat actors monetize by leasing phone numbers and collecting the fraudulent SMS charges.

articles.title: Introducing the New Agentic Architecture for Snyk Agent Fix: Faster, Smarter, and More Secure
articles.summary: Snyk Agent Fix now uses an agentic architecture for AI-driven code remediation with full Snyk Code language coverage and verified fixes. The upgrade enables faster and more reliable automated vulnerability patching across supported languages.

articles.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
articles.summary: Choco deployed OpenAI APIs to build AI agents that streamline food distribution workflows, reducing manual coordination overhead. The case study demonstrates agent-based automation for supply-chain operations.

articles.title: Model Risk Management in 2026: A Banker's Guide to the Revised Interagency Guidance
articles.summary: Databricks provides guidance on updated Federal Reserve model risk management requirements effective April 2026. The post contextualizes regulatory changes for financial institutions deploying AI and ML models.

articles.title: Top 10 uses for Codex at work
articles.summary: Guide covering 10 practical applications of Codex agents for workplace automation, including task execution, document generation, and multi-tool workflows. Intended as a reference for teams evaluating Codex deployment.

articles.title: How to get started with Codex
articles.summary: Step-by-step tutorial for setting up Codex projects, creating threads, and executing first tasks. Introductory documentation for developers new to the Codex platform.

articles.title: Working with Codex
articles.summary: Setup and operational guide for Codex workspaces, including thread and project management, file handling, and step-by-step task execution. Covers the core UX for agent orchestration.

articles.title: Codex settings
articles.summary: Configuration reference for Codex personalization, output detail levels, and permission controls. Enables teams to tailor behavior and security posture for their specific workflows.

articles.title: Scaling the AI-Ready Data Center with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition and NVIDIA vGPU 20
articles.summary: NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell and vGPU 20 enable shared GPU compute across enterprise applications and AI workloads in virtualized environments. The announcement addresses the bottleneck of GPU access for developers by providing secure multi-tenant GPU virtualization for mainstream enterprise and design tools.

articles.title: Making ChatGPT better for clinicians
articles.summary: OpenAI launches free ChatGPT for Clinicians for verified U.S. physicians, nurse practitioners, and pharmacists. Targets clinical documentation, care support, and research workflows.

articles.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
articles.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised a dozen major tech companies and stole tens of millions in cryptocurrency. Buchanan faces over 20 years in prison.

articles.title: QIMMA قِمّة ⛰: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
articles.summary: TIIUAE launches QIMMA, a leaderboard for Arabic LLMs emphasizing quality metrics over raw scale. Includes benchmarks for Arabic language understanding, generation, and cultural relevance.

articles.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
articles.summary: Discusses the role of open-source AI models and transparency in advancing cybersecurity defenses and threat detection. Argues for open collaboration in AI security research over proprietary approaches.

articles.title: Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI
articles.summary: A walkthrough of building a terminal-based emoji list generator using the GitHub Copilot CLI, Copilot SDK, and open-source libraries like opentui/core and clipboardy. The project demonstrates practical use of Copilot's agentic capabilities for rapid CLI tool development.

articles.title: Bringing more transparency to GitHub's status page
articles.summary: GitHub is rolling out three changes to its status page: a new 'Degraded Performance' incident severity level, per-service uptime metrics, and a dedicated 'Copilot AI Model Providers' component for clearer visibility into model provider availability. These changes improve incident classification accuracy and communication granularity.

articles.title: How To Measure the ROI of Developer Tools
articles.summary: Framework for quantifying developer tool ROI through internal surveys, productivity metrics, and team-size-specific approaches, addressing the challenge of justifying platform engineering investments like Backstage beyond subjective experience improvements.

articles.title: Build a personal organization command center with GitHub Copilot CLI
articles.summary: A GitHub staff engineer built a personal command center using the Copilot CLI to unify scattered tools into a single interface, moving from idea to working prototype in a day using AI-assisted planning and implementation. The project demonstrates practical productivity gains from agentic tool-use.
